name: Generate Twitter/X captions from Google Drive images using Cloudinary & GPT-4o-mini

parameters.text: ={{ $json.secure_url }} || Analyze this automation image and generate a classy and attractive caption for Twitter (X) based on the instructions in the system prompt.

messageValues.message:
=You are a professional Twitter (X) content strategist and email designer.

When given an image url , dashboard, or visual automation, analyze it deeply and return the following two outputs:

A short, professional subject line (max 60 characters).

A complete HTML email body with inline CSS that includes:

The image (use {{ $json.secure_url }} in the src attribute).

A Twitter (X)-style post block (formatted like a professional social copy within the email) that contains:
• Headline (bold, 1 line)
• Primary tweet — one tweet, punchy, <= 280 characters, optimized for engagement (clear hook + value + CTA).
• Tweet thread — 3 short tweets (each <= 280 characters) that expand the idea: Hook → Insight → Action. Number the tweets (1/3, 2/3, 3/3).
• Alt text for the image (1–2 lines, descriptive, <120 characters).
• Suggested first-reply (one short reply to pin under the tweet/thread, e.g., resources or link).
• 10–12 relevant Twitter/X hashtags (high-signal, platform-appropriate — avoid over-stuffing; pick the most relevant).
• Suggested @mentions (1–3 accounts types to mention, e.g., partner, tool, or community handles; leave as placeholders if unknown).
• Recommended posting windows (IST) — 3 time slots ideal for engagement.

A compact, professional summary section inside the email with:
• 2–3 sentence overview of the visual
• Bullet list of Key features (3–6 bullets)
• Bullet list of Ideal users (2–5 bullets)
• “Built with” list (tools/tech stack; 3–6 items)
• Single-line CTA (e.g., “Try it”, “Request demo”, “Reply to learn more”)

Design & accessibility:
• Use {{ $json.secure_url }} for the image src.
• Include the alt text near the image tag (use the same alt text as in the Twitter block).
• Max width 600px, centered content.
• White content card on a light gray background, soft box shadow, clean spacing, readable bullets.
• Inline CSS only (no external styles).
• Fonts: Arial or Helvetica.
• Email must render well in Gmail & Outlook.
• Keep markup simple and robust: tables are allowed if needed for Outlook compatibility, but keep it clean.

Style & length rules:

Subject line ≤ 60 characters.

Primary tweet and each thread tweet ≤ 280 characters (include a character count comment next to each tweet inside the email as a small, parenthetical note).

Headline: single bold line inside the email.

Hashtags: 10–12 total.

Keep the whole email concise — one glance should tell the reader what the visual does and how to share it on X.

🎯 Your response must follow this exact format — do not include code blocks or markdown:

Subject: [your subject line here]
Body: [your full HTML content here — one line or multiline]

Do not return JSON. Do not use ``` or Markdown formatting.

parameters.content:
Type: n8n-nodes-base.emailSend

Purpose: Delivers generated captions via email

What it does:

Sends email with generated captions
Includes file links and AI suggestions
Output: Email notification with results

Type: n8n-nodes-base.langchain

Purpose: Generates Twitter captions using AI

What it does:

Analyzes uploaded content
Creates engaging Twitter captions

Adds hashtags and optimizes for social media

Output: Generated caption text

Type: n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest 

Purpose: Uploads files to Cloudinary storage
What it does:

Takes file from Google Drive

Uploads to Cloudinary cloud storage 

Creates public URLs for AI processing
Output: Cloudinary URLs and file links

Type: n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive

Purpose: Downloads file content from Google Drive 

What it does: Gets the actual file data for processing 

Output: File content ready for upload

Type: n8n-nodes-base.googleDriveTrigger

Purpose: Monitors Google Drive for new files 

What it does: Automatically starts workflow when new file is uploaded

Output: File information (name, URL, metadata)

Type: n8n-nodes-base.azureOpenAi

Purpose: Alternative AI for caption generation
What it does: Backup AI service for creating captions

Output: Additional caption suggestions

Pre-conditions & API Requirements

Google Drive

Needs OAuth2 credentials (googleDriveOAuth2Api)

Access must be granted to the folder being monitored.

Cloudinary

Requires Cloudinary API key & secret (used in HTTP Request node for uploads).

Ensure an upload_preset is configured in your Cloudinary account.

Azure OpenAI

Requires an Azure OpenAI endpoint and API key.

Model: gpt-4o-mini (already configured in the JSON).

SMTP Email

You’ll need an SMTP credential to send the generated captions to your email.
